title: Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Thrombocytopenia: ITP, TTP, HUS & DIC requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.
